name: Dairy-Free Veggie Pizza
description: A fully dairy-free pizza: homemade crust, marinara, dairy-free mozzarella, and sauteed mushrooms, peppers, and onion. From The Kitchn's vegan pizza (ingredients captured from search; site is fetch-blocked this session — paste the full card to refine).
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 cups all-purpose flour (plus more for shaping)
1 cup (about 4 oz) shredded dairy-free mozzarella (e.g. Violife or Miyoko's)

recipeInstructions.text: Make the dough: stir yeast into the lukewarm water and let sit until foamy, ~5 min. Mix in flour, salt, and olive oil to form a dough; knead briefly, then cover and let rise until puffy, about 1 hour.
Preheat the oven as hot as it goes (500°F or higher) with a stone or steel if you have one.
Stretch the dough thin on a floured surface or parchment. Spread with pizza sauce, leaving a 1/2-inch border.
Top with the dairy-free mozzarella, mushrooms, bell pepper, and red onion; sprinkle with Italian seasoning.
Bake until the crust is browned and crisp, 13 to 15 minutes. Slice and serve.

author: The Kitchn

ai.cliffNotes: A dairy-free pizza that doesn't taste like a compromise: crisp homemade crust, marinara, melty dairy-free mozz, and sauteed veg.
keyTips: Pre-cook watery vegetables (mushrooms especially) for a couple minutes before topping — raw they release liquid and steam the crust soft.
Dairy-free mozzarella melts better with a thin drizzle of olive oil on top and the hottest oven you have; give it the full bake to brown.
Stretch the dough thin and don't overload the toppings — a loaded dairy-free pie goes soggy fast.
Bake on a preheated stone/steel for the crispest bottom; otherwise preheat the sheet pan.
